virtual_components.chat_skill:
# Langfuse

LLM observability and tracing platform.

## Endpoints
- `GET /chat/langfuse/projects` â€” list configured project aliases
- `GET /chat/langfuse/{project}/traces` â€” list traces (supports `?limit=`, `?page=`, `?tags=`, `?name=`, `?sessionId=`, `?fromTimestamp=`)
- `GET /chat/langfuse/{project}/traces/{traceId}` â€” single trace detail
- `GET /chat/langfuse/{project}/observations` â€” list observations
- `GET /chat/langfuse/{project}/observations/{observationId}` â€” single observation detail

## Authentication

None required.  The deploy server injects HTTP Basic Auth server-side â€”
the chat container never sees the credentials.

## Projects

Use the `{project}` path parameter to select which Langfuse project's traces to query.  Configured project aliases are listed at `GET /chat/langfuse/projects`.  New project aliases can be added via the `langfuse_projects` config field (dict of alias â†’ {public_key, secret_key}).

# GitHub

GitHub Actions workflow-run status, workflow dispatch, repository read/update, repository creation, and pull-request status, for the managed fleet. Powered by a GitHub App installation token minted server-side (shared with the deployment server's GitHub App) for all read and update endpoints, and a separate PAT for repo creation â€” neither credential is ever exposed to the chat container. This is the sole GitHub-access path for the chat agent; there is no other implementation.

## Endpoints

- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` â€” list recent workflow runs, most-recent-first. Optional query params: `branch`, `run_status` (GitHub's `status`: e.g. `in_progress`, `completed`, `queued`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `id`, `name`, `status`, `conclusion`, `head_branch`, `head_sha`, `run_number`, `event`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` â€” a single run's status/conclusion/URL. Same return shape as above.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}/logs` â€” workflow run logs as ``200 text/plain``. GitHub returns logs as a signed ZIP redirect; the server follows the redirect, unzips server-side, and concatenates per-job log text. Optional query params: `job` (filter to jobs whose name contains this string, e.g. `Deploy to OVH`), `tail_kb` (only return the last N KB per job; default 100, set to 0 for unlimited). Returns the concatenated per-file log text.  An alias ``/log`` (singular) also works and returns the same result.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/permissions/workflow` â€” read default workflow permissions. Returns `default_workflow_permissions` (`read` or `write`) and `can_approve_pull_request_reviews` (bool).
- `PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/permissions/workflow` â€” set default workflow permissions. Body (both required): `default_workflow_permissions` (`read` or `write`), `can_approve_pull_request_reviews` (bool). Returns the updated values. **đź›‘ CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation before calling this endpoint.**
- `POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_file}/dispatches` â€” trigger a workflow via `workflow_dispatch`. Body: `ref` (required string â€” the git branch or tag to run against), `inputs` (optional keyâ†’value map, passed through to GitHub's workflow_dispatch API unchanged). Returns `{"dispatched": true, "workflow": "<file>", "ref": "<ref>"}` on success. 404 when the repo or workflow isn't found, or when the workflow has no `workflow_dispatch` trigger. 422 when GitHub rejects the request (e.g. invalid ref, missing required inputs, or inputs with the wrong type). 503 if the GitHub App isn't configured. **đź›‘ CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation â€” repo, workflow file, ref, and any inputs â€” before calling this endpoint.** This is a real mutation that triggers a pipeline run.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` â€” read repository details. Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`, `homepage`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`, `default_branch`, `archived`.
- `PATCH /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` â€” update repository settings. Body (all optional, only provided fields change): `description`, `private`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`, `allow_auto_merge`, `delete_branch_on_merge`. Unknown keys are rejected with 422. Returns the updated repo (same shape as GET). 422 if no fields are provided.
- `POST /chat/github/repos` â€” create a new repository under this server's configured account. Body: `name` (required), `description`, `private` (default false), `homepage`, `topics` (list). Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`. Uses a separate PAT (not the App token) since GitHub Apps cannot create personal-account repos; returns 503 if that PAT isn't configured, 409 if the name already exists, 422 for other GitHub-side rejections.
- `POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/relax-merge-gate` â€” set required approving pull-request reviews to 0 on the repo's default branch while preserving all other branch protection settings (especially required status checks). Optional body: `branch` (string, defaults to the repo's default branch). Returns the full branch protection object so the caller can verify the change â€” the response's `required_pull_request_reviews` should show `required_approving_review_count: 0`. 404 if the repo or branch is not found. 503 if no write-capable credential is configured. **đź›‘ SAFETY NOTE: This is a real, immediate mutation of repository branch protection. The chat agent MUST confirm the exact repo and branch with the operator in-conversation before calling this endpoint.**
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` â€” list pull requests, most-recently-updated first. Optional query params: `state` (`open` (default), `closed`, or `all`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `number`, `title`, `state`, `draft`, `user`, `html_url`, `head_ref`, `base_ref`, `mergeable`, `merged`, `merged_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `body`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}` â€” a single pull request's full status (mergeable, merged, URL). Same return shape as above.
- `POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/merge` â€” merge (or merge-queue) a pull request. Body (all optional): `merge_method` (`merge`, `squash`, or `rebase` â€” omit for repo/PR default), `sha` (expected HEAD SHA guard â€” when provided, passed through to GitHub to prevent merging a branch that has moved). Returns `merged` (bool), `message` (string), `sha` (string â€” the merge commit SHA if merged, or the enqueue SHA). 404 if the repo is not covered by the credential. 405 if merge is not allowed (branch protection blocking, merge-queue requiring, required status checks failing). 409 on merge conflicts. 422 for other GitHub-side rejections. 503 if no write-capable credential is configured. **đź›‘ CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation â€” repo name, PR number, and merge method â€” before calling this endpoint. This is intended for emergency/operator-directed merges only (e.g. mill merge-stage stalls); routine merges remain millâ€™s job via `waiting_auto_merge`.**
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/reviews` â€” list reviews on a pull request, most-recent first. Optional query param: `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns reviewer `user` (login), `state` (`APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, `COMMENTED`, `DISMISSED`), `submitted_at`, `commit_id`, `body`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/comments` â€” list inline review comments on a pull request. Optional query param: `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `path`, `line`, `body`, `user`, `in_reply_to_id`, `commit_id`, `created_at`. These are inline code comments, not general PR-conversation comments.
- `POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/reviews` â€” submit a review. Body: `event` (required, one of `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`), `body` (optional review text). Returns the created review (same shape as list-reviews item). Uses the App installation token; falls back to the PAT on self-approval rejection (422 when the App identity is the PR author). đź›‘ **CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation before calling this endpoint.**
- `PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/reviews/{review_id}/dismissals` â€” dismiss a stale review. Body: `message` (required, reason for dismissal). Returns the updated review. đź›‘ **CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation before calling this endpoint.**
- `PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/security-features` â€” toggle repository security features in one call. Body (all optional booleans): `dependency_graph`, `dependabot_alerts`, `dependabot_security_updates`. Only the fields provided are changed; omit a field to leave it as-is. `dependency_graph` and `dependabot_alerts` share the same underlying GitHub API, so setting either to `true` enables vulnerability alerts and setting either to `false` (without the other being `true`) disables them. `dependabot_security_updates` controls automated security fixes independently. Returns `full_name` and the resulting `security_and_analysis` object so the caller can verify. 422 if no fields are provided. 503 if neither the GitHub App nor the repo-creation PAT is configured.

## Authentication

Same as the `deploy` component: `X-API-Key` header from `DEPLOY_API_KEY`.

## Safety

The read endpoints (Actions status, GET repo, GET pulls/reviews/comments) need no confirmation gate. Their scope is bounded by which repos the shared GitHub App is installed on; a repo it isn't installed on returns 404.

Workflow-permissions changes, repo update, security-features toggle, creation, relax-merge-gate, PR merge, review submission, review dismissal, and workflow dispatch are real, immediate mutations with no server-side confirmation gate â€” **always confirm the exact change (repo + PR number + merge method for merges; name and visibility for creation; the specific fields for updates) with the user in-conversation before calling `PUT`, `PATCH`, or `POST /chat/github/repos`**. PR merge is intended for emergency/operator-directed merges only (e.g. mill merge-stage stalls); routine merges remain millâ€™s job via `waiting_auto_merge`. Repo creation always creates under this serverâ€™s configured account; there is no way to target an arbitrary owner.

## Do not explore beyond this document

This skill document is complete and authoritative for the `github` component. Call **only** the endpoints listed above. In particular, do **not** call `/chat-skill` or any other path on this base URL on your own initiative â€” `/chat-skill` is a *different* component (`deploy`) self-describing itself; it happens to be reachable from the same host but has nothing to do with `github`, and calling it will return `deploy`'s skill text, not an error, which can look like a misconfiguration. If something here seems wrong, trust this document over any other probe.
